displayTitle: Three Levers Between You and the Dose

beats.narration: Every radiation-protection decision comes down to three levers — and every safe job pulls all three, with numbers, not hope.

payload.title: TIME · DISTANCE · SHIELDING
payload.sub: Three levers. Use each one deliberately.

beats.narration: Time: dose equals dose rate times time. Two-forty milliroentgen per hour for a quarter-hour is sixty milliroentgen. Halve the time, halve the dose.

payload.heading: Lever one — time in the field

tiles.s: The field you must work in
Halve the time, halve the dose

beats.narration: Distance is your strongest lever. Radiation spreads like light from a bulb, so intensity falls with the square. Double the distance, quarter the dose rate.

payload.heading: Lever two — distance, by the square
payload.note: DOUBLE THE DISTANCE → ONE QUARTER THE DOSE RATE

beats.narration: Shielding works fractionally. Each half-value layer cuts the rate in half — stack three for one eighth. A collimator aims shielding at the beam.
Then prove it. Survey at the boundary, and trust the interlock: open that door mid-shot and the exposure ends by itself.

payload.sub: If the meter disagrees with the math, the meter wins.
